channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Rescue camera gave officials a view of hundreds of miners in South Africa trapped in shaft
item.description: CARLETONVILLE, South Africa (AP) — A specialized mine rescue camera was lowered late last year into an almost 2.6-kilometer (1.6-mile) deep mineshaft in South Africa, where hundreds of illegal miners were reported to be trapped, starving, dehydrated and desperate to get out.
item.dc:creator: MOGOMOTSI MAGOME, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: White House cheers release of two Americans freed in a swap with Taliban brokered by Biden, Qatar
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A prisoner swap between the United States and Afghanistan's Taliban freed two Americans in exchange for a Taliban figure imprisoned for life in California on drug trafficking and terrorism charges, officials said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER, JON GAMBRELL and AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Middle East latest: Stabbing attack in Israel wounds 4 people, police say
item.description: Four people were wounded in a stabbing attack in central Tel Aviv on Tuesday evening and the suspect was killed, according to Israeli police, as Israel’s fragile ceasefire with Hamas in Gaza entered its third day. Police did not identify the attacker but believe the stabbings were a terrorist act.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: At least 76 people killed in a hotel fire at a ski resort in Turkey
item.description: ANKARA, Turkey (AP) — A fire raged through a 12-story hotel at a popular ski resort in northwestern Turkey early Tuesday during a school holiday, killing at least 76 people — at least two of them when they jumped from the building to escape the flames, officials said.
item.dc:creator: SUZAN FRASER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Mexico defends sovereignty as US seeks to label cartels as terrorists
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — Mexico President Claudia Sheinbaum said Tuesday that President Donald Trump's executive order moving toward designating drug cartels as foreign terrorist organizations would only impact Mexico if there's close coordination between the two governments.
item.dc:creator: MEGAN JANETSKY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Suspected settlers attacked Palestinian villages hours before Trump rescinded Biden sanctions
item.description: JINSAFUT, West Bank (AP) — Shortly after suspected Jewish settlers stormed Palestinian villages in the occupied West Bank late Monday, setting cars and property ablaze, U.S. President Donald Trump canceled sanctions against Israelis accused of violence in the territory.
item.dc:creator: AREF TUFAHA and TIA GOLDENBERG, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Trump mistakes Spain for a member of the BRICS bloc and repeats the threat of massive tariffs
item.description: BARCELONA, Spain (AP) — U.S. President Donald Trump has apparently confused Spain for a member of the BRICS bloc of developing economies, causing some head-scratching and jitters over possible tariffs in Madrid.
item.dc:creator: JOSEPH WILSON, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: 66 dead after fire erupts at popular ski hotel in Turkey
item.description: At least 66 people died and 51 others became injured in a fire that broke out at a Turkish ski resort hotel on Tuesday. Over 200 guests were staying at the hotel when the disaster struck.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israeli intel indicates Hamas held hostages at new Gaza hospital as UN health agency criticized for inaction
item.description: The IDF recently completed a major raid at the Kamal Adwan Hospital in the northern Gaza Strip, arresting some 240 terrorists. Fox News Digital has learned that hostages were reportedly held there.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Same-sex marriages will soon become legal in Thailand after historic law
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — They have been in a committed relationship for more than 13 years, and even had a wedding in 2019. Since then, Danaya Phonphayung and Sunma Piamboon, both women, have considered themselves a married couple, even if same-sex marriages were not legally recognized.
item.dc:creator: JINTAMAS SAKSORNCHAI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: South Korea's Yoon defends his martial law decree in his first public appearance since his arrest
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korea’s impeached president denied Tuesday that he ordered the military to drag lawmakers out of the National Assembly to prevent them from voting to reject his martial law decree last month, as he appeared for the first time before the Constitutional Court that will
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: As Davos 2025 reacts to Trump tack, EU chief leads defense against climate change
item.description: President Donald Trump has given everyone at Davos something to talk about with his actions on the first day of his second term.
item.dc:creator: Jamey Keaten, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: White House cheers release of two Americans freed in a swap with Taliban brokered by Biden, Qatar
item.description: A prisoner swap between the United States and Afghanistan’s Taliban freed two Americans in exchange for a Taliban figure imprisoned for life in California on drug trafficking and terrorism charges.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump wants to pull the US out of the World Health Organization again. Here's what may happen next
item.description: President Donald Trump has used one of the flurry of executive actions that he issued on his first day back in the White House to begin the process of withdrawing the U.S. from the World Health Organization for the second time in less than five years.
item.dc:creator: Maria Cheng And Will Weissert, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Panama, familiar with US intervention, bristles at Trump's comments on canal
item.description: PANAMA CITY (AP) — U.S. President Donald Trump’s insistence Monday that he wants to have the Panama Canal back under U.S. control fed nationalist sentiment and worry in Panama, home to the critical trade route and a country familiar with U.S. military intervention.
item.dc:creator: JUAN ZAMORANO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Stefanik pledges an 'America First' agenda at the UN and a review of US funding
item.description: Rep. Elise Stefanik has pledged to push President Donald Trump’s “America First” stance if confirmed as U.S. ambassador to the United Nations and roll out a review of U.S. funding critical for U.N. agencies.
item.dc:creator: Farnoush Amiri And Edith M. Lederer, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Yemen's Houthis signal they'll now limit their attacks in the Red Sea corridor to Israeli ships
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — Yemen's Houthi rebels signaled Monday they now will limit their attacks in the Red Sea corridor to only Israeli-affiliated ships after a ceasefire began in the Gaza Strip, but warned wider assaults could resume if needed.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump announces second withdrawal from Paris Climate Agreement
item.description: In his first term, Trump announced the pullout in 2017, with the process becoming official two years later.
item.dc:creator: Zack Budryk, NewsNation

item.title: Pope dissolves Peru-based conservative Catholic movement after abuses uncovered by Vatican
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis has taken the remarkable step of dissolving a Peruvian-based Catholic movement, the Sodalitium Christianae Vitae, after years of attempts at reform and a Vatican investigation.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD and FRANKLIN BRICEÑO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Italian journalist detained in Iran says she expected to be held longer and praises Musk's role
item.description: ROME (AP) — An Italian journalist detained in Iran whose fate became intertwined with that of an Iranian engineer wanted by the United States said she assumed she would have been held much longer, and said her boyfriend's contact with Elon Musk might have been “fundamental” to her release.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israel releases 90 Palestinian prisoners as part of cease-fire deal to free hostages
item.description: Israel released 90 Palestinian prisoners to cheering crowds in the West Bank after Hamas returned three hostages that were being held in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: At least 80 people killed in northeast Colombia as peace talks fail, official says
item.description: BOGOTÁ, Colombia (AP) — More than 80 people were killed in the country’s northeast over the weekend following the government's failed attempts to hold peace talks with the National Liberation Army, a Colombian official said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 8 people die in a fire at a home for the elderly in Serbia, officials say
item.description: BELGRADE, Serbia (AP) — Eight people died Monday in a fire at a home for older people on the outskirts of the Serbian capital, Belgrade, that is suspected of being set by a resident, officials said. Seven others were injured in the blaze that erupted around 3:30 a.m.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israeli American hostage father says he has ‘immense gratitude’ for Trump team after ceasefire deal
item.description: An Israeli American hostage’s father said Sunday that he has “immense gratitude” for President-elect Trump’s team in the wake of a ceasefire deal in the Israel-Hamas war going into effect. “You're going to be attending the inauguration tomorrow.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Trump wants to visit China, strengthen relationship: Report
item.description: The president-elect has vowed to impose tariffs and other mechanisms against China upon his return to the White House.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: German ambassador warns Trump will 'undermine' democratic principles with 'maximum disruption' agenda: report
item.description: In a confidential briefing document, Germany's ambassador to the United States Andreas Michaelis warned that Donald Trump will "undermine" democratic principles.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: What to expect as Israel-Hamas cease-fire goes into effect on Sunday
item.description: The Israeli government approved the deal early Saturday morning for a cease-fire in Gaza and the release of hostages captured after Hamas' terrorist attack on Israel on Oct. 7, 2023.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel Defense Forces will receive hostages Sunday with equipped camper trailers and comforting supplies
item.description: The Israel Defense forces completed preparations Saturday to receive 33 hostages being held by Hama Sunday after a cease-fire deal was reached.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: WATCH: Stray dog brings sick, freezing puppy to vet's office
item.description: A stray dog was caught on camera carrying her sick puppy to the front door of a veterinarian clinic in Turkey earlier this week.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Gunman shoots dead 2 Supreme Court judges in Iran's capital before turning gun on himself, state media says
item.description: Two Iranian Supreme Court judges were shot and killed in Tehran on Saturday while a third judge and a bodyguard were wounded, although the motive is unclear.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Cease-fire between Israel, Hamas to go into effect Sunday morning, Qatar says
item.description: The cease-fire between Hamas and Israel is expected to go into effect on Sunday at 8:30 a.m. local time, Qatar’s foreign ministry announced Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pope Francis hurts his right arm after falling for the second time in just over a month
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis fell Thursday and hurt his right arm, the Vatican said, just weeks after another apparent fall resulted in a bad bruise on his chin. Francis didn’t break his arm, but a sling was put on as a precaution, the Vatican spokesman said in a statement On Dec.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Netanyahu says 'last-minute crisis' with Hamas holding up approval of Gaza truce and hostage deal
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu’s office said Thursday his Cabinet won’t meet to approve the agreement for a ceasefire in the Gaza Strip and the release of dozens of hostages until Hamas backs down, accusing the group of reneging on parts of the agreement in an atte
item.dc:creator: TIA GOLDENBERG, WAFAA SHURAFA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Two Americans to be released from Gaza in first phase of ceasefire deal
item.description: Two Americans, Keith Siegel and Sagui Dekel-Chen, are expected to be released as part of the first phase of the Israel-Hamas ceasefire deal that emerged Wednesday after being held for 15-months by Hamas since the start of the war in Gaza, according to a senior administration official.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: Families of hostages grateful but cautious about ceasefire deal
item.description: While some may soon reunite with their loved ones, others face the grim possibility of receiving confirmation of deaths during captivity.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation

item.title: Details need to be addressed in Israel-Hamas ceasefire
item.description: When a ceasefire between Israel and Hamas is finalized depends on the handling of specific implementation details
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, NewsNation

item.title: South Korean authorities take impeached President Yoon to detention center after questioning
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korea's impeached president, Yoon Suk Yeol, was sent to a detention center near Seoul on Wednesday night, after being questioned by anti-corruption officials who took him into custody over his imposition of martial law last month.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, NewsNation
